candidates.stmt:
Rachel is standing as the Green Party candidate in the Ashton ward this May.

Rachel has lived in Ashton for over 20 years and is proud to call it home. Rachel cares deeply about local green spaces, clean streets, and ensuring Ashton remains a great place to live for everyone.

She is standing to help build a fairer, greener Preston, one where local voices are heard and real improvements are made to the issues that matter day to day.

If elected, Rachel will work hard, listen to residents, and stand up for Ashton.

Robin is a 50-year-old father of one. He grew up in Leicestershire and moved to Preston at 19 to attend UCLAN, and has now spent the majority of his life in or near the city.

He works full-time in the city centre and is the Event Director for the local junior parkrun, as well as a regular participant in the Saturday parkrun, often spending time in Avenham Park. Robin also enjoys cooking and repurposing old gadgets rather than letting them go to waste.

Jonathan Grisdale, known as Jono (as named by his mum), is 57 and works in the NHS. An electrician by trade, he is currently seconded into Trade Union Partnership Officer roles.

He lives with his partner Vicky, who is the mother of his six-year-old daughter, Holly, and is father to two children, Theo, 25, and Holly. In his spare time, Jono enjoys craft beers, BMX biking, and cooking.

Tina is a lifelong resident of Preston and has been a Broadgate local for the past five years.

She is an avid board game enthusiast and a regular at a local Preston gaming group. Tina is also an active member of the Typhoons RUFC touch rugby team.

Tina is ready to be a fresh, local voice within the City Council, working to protect our environment and build a sustainable Preston for all.

Dr Kevin Rigotti has lived in Deepdale since 2014. He originally came to Lancashire for a role reviewing aircraft safety evidence and decided to stay in Preston to pursue a research degree at the University of Lancashire.

A member of the Preston Green Party since 2018, Kevin has supported successful campaigns in North Herefordshire, Worcestershire, Cheshire, and Manchester.

Chris was born and raised in Preston and has lived in the city for most of his life. He recently graduated from UCLan with an MSc in Psychology, where he also served as vice-chair of the Neurodivergent Society.

He currently works as a support worker for people with learning disabilities and is passionate about community investment, protecting green spaces, and ensuring residents from all backgrounds are properly represented and listened to.

In his spare time, Chris enjoys spending time with his dogs, Annie and Rusty, as well as playing chess, watching documentaries and sports, listening to music, gaming, and video editing.

Helen is a long-time member of the Green Party and has been standing in local elections for around 15 years. She has also served as a nominating officer and elections agent for over a decade.

She has worked in both education and the charity sector, bringing a wealth of experience to her role. Helen lived in Fulwood for 30 years and having recently moved, she is now standing in Garrison.

Helen is passionate about protecting local green spaces and ensuring that local voices are heard and properly represented.

Jacob has spent most of his life in Preston and has enjoyed volunteering and working with the Lancashire Wildlife Trust. He is currently finishing his PhD in Chemistry at the University of Southampton.

In his spare time, Jacob enjoys hiking and amateur wildlife photography, reflecting his passion for nature. He looks forward to getting involved and supporting the local community.

Toby is relatively new to Preston, having moved to the city nearly two years ago. He has quickly grown to love it, describing it as a place that’s small enough to foster strong communities, yet large enough to offer plenty to do.

In his day job, Toby works as a welder fabricator in motorsport in nearby Leyland. He is a big sports fan and enjoys taking part whenever he can.

Keith is standing in the hope of promoting kinder and more supportive policies. He believes society should help those who are struggling, and that we all have a role to play in offering support.

He began his career as an engineer for Leyland Trucks, later retraining as a maths teacher, and spent the last 20 years of his paid working life supporting families with preschool children. Keith has lived in Preston and Leyland since 1984 and has been a resident of the Lea and Larches ward since 2014.

Since retiring, he has volunteered by giving talks in schools on kindness to animals and personal safety, and he currently facilitates a mental health support group in Preston.

Gabie is Preston’s candidate for Plungington in the 2026 Local Elections. She works in retail at a petrol station and is also a student. Gabie holds a bachelor’s degree in Media and Communication combined with Religious Studies and is currently pursuing a master’s degree in Writing.

A member of the Green Party since November 2025, Gabie believes strongly that politics can improve the lives of local people. If elected, she will work tirelessly to advocate for the residents of Plungington.

My family has lived in Plungington for more than 50 years, and I’m proud to call
it home.

For the last four years I’ve served as your local councillor, and I’m seeking your
support to re-elect me on 7 May.

Councillors connect communities to the council and its services. That’s why I put all my effort into monthly meetings with residents at Eldon Primary, Plungington Community Centre and Noorani Masjid, as well as ward walkabouts with fellow Labour councillors, council staff, and police.

**A record of delivering for Plungington**
Working with residents, your Labour councillors have:
- Secured action on anti-social behaviour, drug-related crime
- Delivered cleaner streets, tackled 600 cases of fly-tipping
- Got £4M funds to upgrade Moor Park café, football pitches, and pond
- Opened new playgrounds at Brook St Rec and Moor Park 
- Introduced stricter licensing rules to stop bad landlords

**A record of supporting residents**
During the cost-of-living crisis, Preston Labour has delivered real help to residents:
- Up to £400 cost-of-living help for families 
- Council tax support for pensioners 
- Free swimming for young people and low-income residents 
- Investment in community projects and youth services

Please vote for me on 7 May so that I can continue working hard for you.

Pav

TUSC is a group of workers, trade unionists, young people, older people, students, ethnic and religious minorities, etc. I am one of many young students with working class parents just trying to get by, that is standing and campaigning for TUSC, because we're fighting for protections to the NHS, fighting against cuts to vital areas of funding that other brilliant and hard working workers need such as the winter fuel allowance, disability protections and school funding. We stand as members of worker's and student unions because we know the other parties are all funded by rich ideologues who do not represent the average working person. As such, we call for no more wasting our taxpayer money on frivolous pursuits while cutting funding to essential services, no more privatising, no more pandering to big businesses, no more attacks on the good working and middle class people of this country, no more harming our natural environment, no more to genocide in Gaza, and no more to the Tories, Labour, LibDems, Reform or the Greens, all of whom vote in favour of austerity and anti-union laws. Vote for TUSC where and when you can, as a workers' alternative for a workers' Britain.

Karen grew up in Longridge and returned there six years ago. She is a co-chair of the Longridge Environment Group, a student of sustainability, and a parent.

Concerned about the future, Karen highlights the interconnected crises of climate, nature, and inequality, which are already having real and profound effects on people’s lives.

She is committed to working locally to do whatever she can to build a brighter, fairer future where people and the planet can thrive together.

My name is Danny, and I’m proud to be standing as your Green Party candidate for the Preston Rural North ward this May. Offering a positive alternative to the usual politics.

I live in the ward and work in Preston, so this community isn’t just where I’m standing, it’s my home. I care deeply about the area, the people who live here, and the issues that affect our day-to-day lives.

I’m standing because I believe we can do better: protecting our local environment, improving services, supporting local businesses, and ensuring residents’ voices are properly heard in decision-making.

I’m particularly passionate about protecting our local green spaces and making sure new developments are carefully planned, with the infrastructure needed to support them. Rural areas like ours should not be overlooked when it comes to investment and services.

Thank you for taking the time to read this. I hope to earn your support.

Dan moved to Preston in 2022 from Worcestershire. He is severely sight impaired and has been involved in campaigning for disability equality throughout his life.

He has been an activist within the Green Party since 2014, as well as a trade union steward for most of the time since 2012. While not originally from Preston, Dan has grown to love the area he lives in and the wider city.

He is committed to making life better in any way he can – from challenging local issues to taking practical steps such as litter picking and ensuring problems that need reporting are addressed.

Amy was born and raised in Preston and has lived in the Sharoe Green ward for the past 14 years with her partner and son, who attends a local high school.

A long time employee of the NHS, Amy worked for many years at the Royal Preston Hospital before specialising in mental health.

Amy cares deeply about her community, and helping people is at the heart of everything she does.

I’m proud to be standing as your Labour candidate in St Matthew’s on 7 May.

St Matthew’s is a community I care about deeply. My grandparents settled on Skeffington Rd when they came to the UK 60 years ago. My family live off Ribbleton Lane, and my child attends Quwwatul Islam on Peel Hall St. 
 
In my day job, as an NHS nurse, I see the pressure people are facing every day and how public services improve people’s lives. I’m active in the community, volunteering and advocating for women and disabled people. I’m also proud to support a  faith-based charity that helps people in Preston.

**Record of delivering for St Matthew’s**
We all know St Matthew’s is a caring community to live in, but residents raise concerns about fly-tipping, littering and community safety. These are all issues our local Labour team has been working on with residents. Success so far includes:
- Secured CCTV on key streets and increased police presence
- Tackled over 700 fly-tipping cases with stronger enforcement
- Secured additional street cleaning and environmental improvements

**Supporting people in St Matthew’s**
- Preston Labour has been delivering real support for our residents:
- Cost-of-living help, including up to £400 for struggling families
- Council tax support for pensioners
- Free swimming for children, young people and those on low incomes

Elect me to continue working on these issues and ensure residents are heard at the Town Hall. I won’t let you down.

Takhsin

Gareth was born and raised in Preston, with strong roots in St Matthew’s Ward, particularly the Ribbleton area. He attended St Matthew’s Primary School, and his mum still lives in his childhood home.

He has spent 20 years working in social housing and now works as a Senior Consultant specialising in Fire and Gas Safety. Gareth is passionate about safe homes, strong communities, and making a meaningful difference through his work.

Gareth lives with his partner and their two beagles. In his spare time, he enjoys cooking, travelling, gaming, listening to true crime podcasts, and enjoying the emotional rollercoaster of supporting Preston North End.
